instructions: (Claude Code's built-in system prompt; see --bare mode docs)

queries.user_text:
You're debugging a real-codebase bug in `yara-1` (project `yara`, an open-source C/C++ project from the BugsC++ corpus).

The buggy source tree is on the host at `/Users/ibraheemamin/projects/COS484/chatdbgpro/data/workspaces/yara-1/yara/buggy-1` and is also mounted at `/work` inside a Linux/amd64 container named `bench-t4-04f1607f04d448d8af6c` (gdb, the buggy binary, and all build deps live in that container; the binary is NOT runnable on this host directly).

Buggy binary inside the container: `/work/(see /work for binary)`
Failing test invocation:           `bash -c bash -c 'echo return 55 > tests/defects4cpp.lua' && make -j1 check`
Observed behavior:                  `exit_code:2`

How to investigate
------------------
* To READ source code, use Read / Grep / your normal tools — the source tree is at `/Users/ibraheemamin/projects/COS484/chatdbgpro/data/workspaces/yara-1/yara/buggy-1` (it's been added via `--add-dir`).
* To RUN the binary, run gdb, or execute anything else inside the build environment, use Bash with this template:

      docker exec -i -w /work bench-t4-04f1607f04d448d8af6c bash -c '<cmd>'

  e.g.
      docker exec -w /work bench-t4-04f1607f04d448d8af6c bash -c 'ls -la'
      docker exec -w /work bench-t4-04f1607f04d448d8af6c bash -c 'gdb -batch -ex run -ex bt --args bash -c bash -c 'echo return 55 > tests/defects4cpp.lua' && make -j1 check'

  The container is dedicated to this case; it'll be torn down when this session ends, so don't worry about cleanup.

Final answer
------------
Identify the root cause in the source, propose both a local fix and a structural global fix.

Your final response MUST include three labelled paragraphs:

  ROOT CAUSE: <file:line and what is wrong>
  LOCAL FIX:  <minimal code change>
  GLOBAL FIX: <structural change preventing this CLASS of bug>

Do NOT modify the source files on disk. Just investigate and produce the diagnosis as your final assistant message.

queries.response:
I now have the complete picture. Here's the diagnosis:

---

**ROOT CAUSE:** `libyara/parser.c:733` — the buggy commit changed `yr_hash_table_lookup(compiler->objects_table, identifier, NULL)` to `yr_hash_table_lookup(compiler->objects_table, identifier, compiler->current_namespace->name)`. External variables (defined via `yr_compiler_define_boolean_variable` and friends) are stored in the hash table under the `NULL` namespace key (global scope). Passing `compiler->current_namespace->name` instead of `NULL` means the lookup only searches within the rule's namespace, so it never finds the externally-defined variable "test" when checking for naming conflicts. As a result, compiling a rule whose name collides with an external variable no longer raises `ERROR_DUPLICATED_IDENTIFIER` — the compiler returns `ERROR_SUCCESS` (0) instead. `test_issue_920()` (selected by `defects4cpp.lua`'s `return 55`) detects this failure and exits with status 1.

**LOCAL FIX:** Restore the second lookup's namespace argument back to `NULL`:
```c
// libyara/parser.c, line 730–733
yr_hash_table_lookup(
    compiler->objects_table,
    identifier,
    NULL) != NULL)   // was: compiler->current_namespace->name
```
This restores the global-scope search for external variables, so a rule name that duplicates any external variable identifier is correctly rejected.

**GLOBAL FIX:** Introduce a dedicated helper `yr_compiler_identifier_exists(compiler, id)` that encapsulates the two-lookup logic (rules table in the current namespace **and** objects table in the global/NULL namespace). All callers in the parser that check for duplicate identifiers should use this helper instead of open-coding separate `yr_hash_table_lookup` calls. This prevents future callers from accidentally passing the wrong namespace argument to either lookup, and makes the differing namespace semantics (current namespace for rules, NULL/global for external variables) explicit and centrally documented.

tool_calls.call: docker exec -w /work bench-t4-04f1607f04d448d8af6c bash -c 'make -j1 check 2>&1 | tail -50'
docker exec -w /work bench-t4-04f1607f04d448d8af6c bash -c 'git diff HEAD~1 HEAD -- libyara/include/yara/error.h 2>&1 | head -80'
docker exec -w /work bench-t4-04f1607f04d448d8af6c bash -c 'git diff HEAD~1 HEAD --name-only 2>&1'
